danger_signals.visual_form: Character falls to ground; knockdown counter increments
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; three knockdowns eliminate player in Grudge Match

danger_signals.visual_form: Crowd members enter arena to attack or push players
Mysterious masked fighter taunt prompts between matches
danger_signals.meaning: Final boss antagonist; ultimate challenge ahead

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary damage reduction and power boost

safety_signals.visual_form: Grudge Match completion; bonus money awarded

safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression to next opponent; advancement in tournament

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct character models for each fighter type (wrestler, kickboxer, martial artist)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arena-based combat setting; pit fighting venue

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eight main opponents with distinct visual identities: Executioner, Southside Jim, Angel, C.C. Rider, Mad Miles, Heavy Metal, Chainman Eddie, Masked Warrior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Weapon-wielders (Knife Woman, Knife Man); crowd-affiliated opponents

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic crowd members capable of combat interference

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard arena configuration; basic opponent roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents with standard combat capabilities; minimal crowd interference
Player fights CPU clone (single-player) or other players (multiplayer); three-knockdown elimination rule active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Masked Warrior antagonist revealed as final opponent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiplayer players must eliminate each other before fighting final boss; mysterious entity taunts throughout tournament

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crowd drops weapons into arena; power pill appears as collectible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Crowd interference escalates; environmental hazards become active combat factors

environmental_storytelling.example: Opponents named after archetypes (Executioner, Southside Jim, Mad Miles, Heavy Metal, Chainman Eddie) communicate fighter specialties and danger levels through nomenclature
Live-action bluescreen captured footage creates visual authenticity and presence; humanoid opponents communicate physical threat through realistic motion capture
Interactive audience attacking players, dropping weapons, and pushing fighters communicates underground pit fighting venue culture and unpredictability
Periodic taunts between matches from mysterious final boss establish narrative tension and establish tournament hierarchy
Playable fighters represent distinct martial traditions (American wrestler, African-American kickboxer, Japanese martial artist) through visual design and move sets

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Big and strong ex-professional wrestler; high power, slower speed
Agile kickboxing champion; high speed, agile movement
Quick 3rd degree black belt; fast martial arts techniques
Periodically taunts player between matches; communicates ultimate antagonist role
Final championship match opponent; mysterious identity; ultimate challenge
Attack players, drop weapons into arena, push stray fighters back into combat
Falls to ground; vulnerable to continued attack; increments knockdown counter

notes: Pit-Fighter communicates world-state primarily through character archetypes, opponent roster progression, and environmental interference mechanics. The digitized live-action graphics establish visual authenticity for a dangerous underground fighting venue. Danger is communicated through opponent names (Executioner, Mad Miles, Chainman Eddie), crowd interference dynamics, and the mysterious Masked Warrior antagonist. Political/faction dynamics are minimal; the game focuses on individual fighter identity through martial tradition archetypes (wrestler, kickboxer, martial artist) rather than faction allegiance. The tournament structure itself communicates progression and hierarchy through match sequencing and boss difficulty escalation.
